expectations.text: uses-agile-sprint-endpoint — Uses /rest/agile/1.0/board/{boardId}/sprint to find the active sprint (not fields.sprint or a custom field)
expectations.evidence: Response uses JIRA CLI command 'jira sprint list --board <BOARD_ID>' instead of the raw REST API endpoint /rest/agile/1.0/board/{boardId}/sprint. No direct REST API call shown.

expectations.text: uses-issue-create-api — Creates the issue via POST /rest/api/3/issue with issuetype, priority, and project key in the request body
expectations.evidence: Response uses JIRA CLI command 'jira issue create' with flags like --type, --priority instead of showing the raw REST API POST /rest/api/3/issue with JSON body containing issuetype, priority, project key.

expectations.text: assigns-to-sprint-correctly — Assigns the issue to the sprint via POST /rest/agile/1.0/sprint/{sprintId}/issue (not by setting a sprint custom field)
expectations.evidence: Response uses JIRA CLI command 'jira sprint add <sprint-id> ENG-427' instead of the raw REST API endpoint POST /rest/agile/1.0/sprint/{sprintId}/issue.

expectations.text: priority-field-correct — Sets priority using the correct JIRA API format: {"priority": {"name": "High"}}
expectations.evidence: Response uses CLI flag '--priority High' in 'jira issue create' command. Does not show the REST API JSON format with {"priority": {"name": "High"}}.

expectations.text: issuetype-field-correct — Sets issuetype using correct format: {"issuetype": {"name": "Bug"}}
expectations.evidence: Response uses CLI flag '--type Bug' in 'jira issue create' command. Does not show the REST API JSON format with {"issuetype": {"name": "Bug"}}.

expectations.text: auth-guidance-present — Includes guidance on authentication (JIRA_TOKEN env var or Authorization header with Basic base64(email:email:token))
expectations.evidence: Response includes: 'export JIRA_API_TOKEN="your-api-token"' and 'jira init --auth-type basic' with email parameter. Mentions API tokens from https://id.atlassian.net → Security → API tokens. Covers JIRA_TOKEN env var and basic auth guidance.

expectations.text: step-sequence-correct — Steps are in correct order: (1) find sprint ID, (2) create issue, (3) assign to sprint
expectations.evidence: Response follows correct sequence: Step 2 finds active sprint ID via 'jira sprint list', Step 3 creates the bug ticket via 'jira issue create', Step 4 assigns to sprint via 'jira sprint add'. Sequence is correct.

expectations.text: uses-correct-jira-domain — Uses the provided site domain mycompany.atlassian.net in the API URLs
expectations.evidence: Response includes 'jira init --server https://mycompany.atlassian.net' on line 23, correctly using the provided domain mycompany.atlassian.net in the API configuration.
